mission.title: Install and Configure Kaito on Kubernetes
mission.description: KAITO is a Kubernetes AI Toolchain Operator that automates the AI/ML model inference or tuning workload in a Kubernetes cluster. It simplifies the onboarding of large AI inference models by providing preset configurations and support for popular inference runtimes.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KAITO Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kaito https://kaito-project.github.io/kaito
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your local Helm chart repository cache to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KAITO using Helm
steps.description:
Install the KAITO operator in the `kaito` namespace (it will be created if it does not exist) using the following command:
```bash
helm install kaito kaito/workspace --namespace kaito --create-namespace --version 0.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the KAITO pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kaito
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KAITO release using Helm:
```bash
helm uninstall kaito --namespace kaito
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Remove any KAITO-related CRDs that were installed:
```bash
kubectl delete crd workspaces.kaito.io
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the `kaito` namespace to remove all associated resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kaito
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, backup the current state of the KAITO operator:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kaito -o yaml > kaito-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the KAITO installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the KAITO operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade kaito kaito/workspace --namespace kaito --version 0.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the KAITO pods are running successfully after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kaito
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
This may occur due to insufficient resources. Check the events for the namespace:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kaito
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) available.

troubleshooting.title: KAITO operator pod crashes
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the KAITO operator pod for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <kaito-pod-name> --namespace kaito
``` 
Look for any error messages that may indicate misconfigurations or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues with CRDs, ensure they are installed correctly:
```bash
kubectl get crds | grep kaito
``` 
If they are missing, reinstall the KAITO operator.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KAITO will show the KAITO operator pod running in the `kaito` namespace, and you will be able to manage AI/ML workloads through the defined custom resources. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and the Helm CLI installed.
